source_label: Head & neck (lip, oral cavity, pharynx; C00-C14)
note: head and neck cancer, broader than the source: nasal-cavity, eye, ear, skull & salivary-gland cancers roll up but are NOT in C00-C14 figures; larynx (C32) is mapped separately
includes intrahepatic bile ducts; HCC and other liver cancers roll up here
malignant pancreatic neoplasm (broad); all pancreatic subtypes roll up
laryngeal carcinoma (no broader laryngeal-cancer MONDO node exists)
lung cancer; pools NSCLC and SCLC (whose prognosis differs) -- rollup pages must say so
all-melanoma node; C43 is cutaneous only (>90% of melanoma). Mucosal/uveal melanoma would roll up here despite being outside the source
uterine cancer; endometrial + carcinosarcoma/sarcoma roll up. The MONDO node also subsumes cervix, but closest-wins keeps cervix on C53
kidney cancer; renal cell carcinoma rolls up here
urinary bladder cancer
CNS cancer; brain, brain glioma and glioblastoma roll up here (the narrower brain-cancer node excludes GBM)

lymphoma (Hodgkin + non-Hodgkin). MONDO nests plasma cell myeloma under 'lymphoma', so myeloma rolls up to these figures for mortality (labelled as broader); Eurostat has no separate myeloma rate -- C90 sits in the unmappable C88_C90_C96 grab-bag
unmappable: an ICD grab-bag (myeloma C90 + immunoproliferative C88 + other C96) with no single MONDO entity -- a specific page must not claim these mixed figures

uterine cancer (corpus; incl. sarcomas/carcinosarcoma)

Hodgkin lymphoma (no leukemia overlap). Non-Hodgkin lymphoma is deliberately NOT mapped: MONDO dual-classifies lymphoid leukemias (CLL, ALL) under BOTH leukemia and NHL, so an NHL site would tie them and drop their leukemia survival
